# User and Host Management

## TUMonline Overview

**TUMonline** is TUM's central campus management system and the primary platform for managing users, groups, and organizational data. It provides a comprehensive interface for IT administrators to manage their department's IT resources.

### Access Information

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| **URL** | [campus.tum.de](https://campus.tum.de) |
| **Access** | Role-based permissions for authorized administrators |
| **Documentation** | TUM Wiki, LRZ Documentation |

## Core Functions

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "TUMonline Core Functions for IT Admins"
mindmap
    root((TUMonline))
        User Management
            Account Administration
            Access Permissions
            Identity Management
        Group Management
            Organizational Groups
            Mailing Lists
            Permission Sets
        Function Objects
            Shared Mailboxes
            Group Calendars
            Service Addresses
        Integration
            LDAP Directory
            Active Directory
            Exchange
```

## User Management

### User Account Lifecycle

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "User Account Lifecycle"
sequenceDiagram
    participant Admin as Administrator
    participant TUMo as TUMonline
    participant LDAP as TUM LDAP / AD
    participant User as New User

    Admin->>TUMo: Manage user account
    TUMo->>TUMo: Process changes
    TUMo->>LDAP: Synchronize identity
    LDAP-->>TUMo: Confirmation
    TUMo-->>Admin: Account updated
    Admin->>User: Communicate access details
    User->>LDAP: Login with TUM credentials
```

User management tasks include:

1. Managing user accounts and permissions via TUMonline
2. Assigning users to organizational groups
3. Configuring access levels for departmental resources
4. Handling account changes when users join or leave

### Managing User Groups

Groups in TUMonline serve multiple purposes:

| Group Type | Purpose | Example Use |
|------------|---------|-------------|
| **Organizational Groups** | Map organizational structure | Department members |
| **Access Groups** | Control resource access | Printer access, file shares |
| **Mailing Lists** | Email distribution | Department announcements |
| **LDAP Groups** | Directory integration | Authentication and authorization |

#### Creating Groups

1. Navigate to Group Management in TUMonline
2. Select "Create New Group"
3. Define group properties:
   - Group name (following naming conventions)
   - Description
   - Initial members
   - Group type
4. Save and activate the group

### LDAP Integration

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "LDAP Integration Flow"
flowchart LR
    TUMo["TUMonline"]
    TUMo --> |sync| LDAP["TUM Central LDAP"]
    LDAP --> Auth["Authentication<br/>Services"]
    LDAP --> NAS["NAS Access<br/>Control"]
    LDAP --> AD["Active<br/>Directory"]
    LDAP --> Mail["Mail<br/>Services"]
```

Groups and users managed through TUMonline are automatically:

- Synchronized with the central TUM LDAP directory
- Available for authentication across TUM IT services
- Used for access control on storage, servers, and other resources

## Host Management

### Registering Devices

Administrators manage device registration for network access:

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "Device Registration Workflow"
flowchart TB
    subgraph Registration["Device Registration"]
        Device["New Device"]
        Device --> Info["Collect Information"]
        Info --> |MAC Address<br/>Device Type<br/>Location| Register["Network Registration"]
    end

    subgraph Configuration["Network Configuration"]
        Register --> DNS["DNS Entry"]
        Register --> DHCP["DHCP Reservation"]
        Register --> Inventory["Inventory Record"]
    end
```

#### Required Information for Host Registration

| Field | Description | Example |
|-------|-------------|---------|
| **Hostname** | Unique device name | `ws-mueller-01` |
| **MAC Address** | Network interface ID | `00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E` |
| **Device Type** | Category | Laptop, Desktop, Printer, Server |
| **Location** | Physical location | Room 01.234, Building |
| **Responsible Person** | Primary user/contact | Prof. Dr. Schmidt |

### Infrastructure Inventory

Administrators should maintain a complete inventory of:

- **Laptops**: Mobile workstations assigned to users
- **Desktops**: Fixed workstations in offices and labs
- **Printers**: Network printers and multifunction devices
- **Servers**: Both virtual and physical servers
- **Network Equipment**: Managed switches and access points

## Email Services

### TUM Email Address

All TUM members (students, employees, guests) automatically receive a personal TUM email address:

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Availability** | Automatic for all TUM members |
| **Lifetime** | Can be used as forwarding address for life |
| **Format** | firstname.lastname@tum.de |

### Exchange Services

Microsoft Exchange provides comprehensive email and collaboration:

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "Exchange Service Features"
mindmap
    root((Exchange))
        Email
            Personal Mailbox
            Shared Mailboxes
            Distribution Lists
        Calendar
            Personal Calendar
            Group Calendars
            Room Booking
        Collaboration
            Shared Contacts
            Tasks
            Notes
```

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Email** | Full email functionality |
| **Calendar** | Personal and shared calendars |
| **Shared Mailboxes** | Team email addresses |
| **Access** | Outlook, Web, Mobile |

### Mailing Lists (Mailverteiler)

Automated mailing list management options:

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| **TUMonline Groups** | Lists based on organizational groups (auto-updated) |
| **Manual Lists** | Traditional managed lists |
| **Dynamic Lists** | Auto-updated based on criteria |

::: {.callout-tip}
## Automatic List Management
Use TUMonline-based mailing lists to avoid manual maintenance. Lists update automatically as people join or leave organizational units.
:::

### Shared Mailboxes and Calendars

Create team collaboration spaces via TUMonline Function Objects (Funktionsobjekte):

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Shared Mailboxes** | Team email addresses (e.g., info@department.tum.de) |
| **Group Calendars** | Shared scheduling |
| **Management** | Via TUMonline Function Objects |

### Mailing Lists vs Shared Mailboxes Comparison

| Feature | Mailing List | Shared Mailbox |
|---------|--------------|----------------|
| **Receive emails** | All members | All members |
| **Send as shared address** | No | Yes |
| **Shared inbox** | No | Yes |
| **Best for** | Announcements | Team collaboration |

## TUMonline Integration

### Overview

TUMonline is TUM's campus management system and integrates with many IT services:

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "TUMonline Service Integration"
flowchart TB
    TUMonline["TUMonline"]

    TUMonline --> Identity["Identity Management"]
    TUMonline --> Email["Email Services"]
    TUMonline --> Groups["Group Management"]
    TUMonline --> Functions["Function Objects"]

    Identity --> AD["Active Directory"]
    Identity --> LDAP["LDAP Directory"]
    Email --> Exchange["Exchange"]
    Groups --> Lists["Mailing Lists"]
    Functions --> Shared["Shared Mailboxes"]
```

### Function Objects (Funktionsobjekte)

TUMonline Function Objects enable creation of:

- Shared mailboxes
- Group calendars
- Team collaboration spaces
- Service desk addresses

### Administrator Resources

| Resource | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| **TUMonline Portal** | User and group management |
| **TUMonline Documentation** | Guides and tutorials |
| **TUMonline-Beauftragte** | Local TUMonline coordinators |
| **Keyuser Areas** | Specialized management areas |

## Best Practices

### User Account Management

- **Regular Audits**: Periodically review active accounts
- **Timely Deactivation**: Remove accounts when users leave
- **Appropriate Permissions**: Apply principle of least privilege
- **Documentation**: Keep records of special access grants

### Group Management

- **Naming Conventions**: Follow established naming patterns
- **Clear Descriptions**: Document group purpose and membership criteria
- **Regular Review**: Audit group memberships periodically
- **Cleanup**: Remove obsolete groups

### Infrastructure Records

- **Complete Information**: Fill all relevant fields
- **Current Data**: Update records when devices move or change
- **Disposal Tracking**: Mark devices as decommissioned when appropriate
